authoritative_sources.title: Treasury Financial Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Bureau of the Fiscal Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The TFM is Treasury's official financial-management manual for federal accounting, reporting, disbursing, collections, payment-related activities, deposits, receivables, and fiscal matters.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public-money, disbursement, collections, reporting, and payment-related handoff classification
used_for: certifying, disbursing, accounting, reporting, and payment-related owner routing
refusal of payment release, certification, and official financial action

authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-11, Preparation, Submission, and Execution of the Budget
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB publishes Circular A-11 as official budget preparation and execution guidance, including apportionment, budget execution, and reporting posture for federal agencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Budget execution, apportionment, reporting, and funding-control dependency review
used_for: budget approval and reprogramming boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-123, Management's Responsibility for Internal Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB Circular A-123 is official OMB guidance for agency internal control and management accountability, making it authoritative for control-owner, documentation, and escalation posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Internal-control, control-owner, corrective-action, and financial-control handoff review
used_for: segregation between coordination and approval authority

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 2 CFR Part 200 is the government-wide federal award rule for financial management, records retention, procurement standards, remedies, hearings, appeals, closeout, and audit requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal award, grant drawdown, procurement dependency, closeout, record retention, and audit readiness review
used_for: records retention and procurement dependency constraints
remedy, objection, hearing, appeal, closeout, and audit stop conditions

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 4.801 - Government Contract Files
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAR 4.801 is authoritative for government contract-file documentation and requires records sufficient to provide a complete transaction history for decisions, reviews, investigations, litigation, and congressional inquiries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement-dependent payment, closeout, purchase request, and contract-file handoff review
used_for: routing when procurement approval or award authority is requested

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority and publishes official records-management guidance, schedules, disposition, transfer, oversight, and reporting resources.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official-record location, retrieval, retention, transfer, disposition, and audit-trail review

authoritative_sources.title: FOIA.gov - Freedom of Information Act
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Office of Information Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FOIA.gov is the official central FOIA website and is authoritative for public access, disclosure, exemption, partial disclosure, and transparency caveats involving agency records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transparency, public-records, privacy, and public-finance record release routing review
used_for: routing to public records or privacy owners

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the federal internal-control standard and is authoritative for documentation, control activities, segregation of duties, monitoring, and auditability expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control, segregation-of-duty, evidence, monitoring, and auditability review
used_for: segregation between handoff support and approval authority

authoritative_sources.title: GAO Appropriations Law Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's appropriations-law resources are authoritative public references for fiscal-law and Antideficiency Act concepts that constrain public-finance coordination, while final legal interpretation remains outside the lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Appropriation, obligation, expenditure, funding-control, and fiscal-law boundary review
used_for: refusal of legal interpretation and unsupported financial approval

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 205.10 - Hearings
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 45 CFR 205.10 is a federal public-assistance hearing rule and is authoritative for notice, opportunity to be heard, and fair-hearing boundaries when finance coordination affects public benefits or services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Rights-sensitive eligibility, notice, hearing, and benefit-payment exception escalation
used_for: boundary between coordination and official benefits adjudication

authoritative_sources.title: Leading Practices in Collaboration Across Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's collaboration practices are authoritative public references for clarifying outcomes, accountability, roles, participants, information sharing, and written agreements across public-sector coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-team coordination, owner clarification, blocker escalation, routing, and closure confirmation
used_for: duplicate ownership and fragmentation failure modes

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, jurisdiction, program, funding source, fiscal year, public-service population, intended action, prior owner, candidate next owner, authority boundary, tenant source hierarchy, and systems-of-record context before routing.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Official record and artifact validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm budget execution references, award files, obligation or commitment records, purchase requests, contract files, invoices, vouchers, case or eligibility records, notices, correspondence, retention metadata, transparency logs, and audit trails are present and traceable.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Owner, system, and blocker reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Compare financial management, budget, grants, procurement, case, records, document, audit, and transparency systems to identify current owner, stale ownership, duplicate ownership, due-date conflicts, retrieval gaps, and blocker state.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Handoff package and routing confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare a handoff summary naming prior owner, next owner, required artifact set, source-system links, assumptions, blocker state, communication obligations, and acceptance criteria without approving the underlying finance action.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Authority, rights, and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop or route when the outcome depends on financial approval, certifying or disbursing authority, procurement approval, legal interpretation, appropriations-law interpretation, program adjudication, eligibility, public-records release, audit finding, enforcement, notice, hearing, or appeal rights.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and audit trail update
workflow_stages.description: Confirm work landed with the destination owner only when owner acceptance, required artifact completeness, unresolved blocker state, source freshness, and audit trail references are recorded; otherwise issue a blocked-dependency escalation.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

artifacts: budget execution records, apportionment or allotment references, fund citations, commitments, obligations, encumbrances, SF-133 or equivalent reporting references, and authority matrices
grant award files, subaward records, drawdown requests, reimbursement packages, closeout records, notices of award, and federal financial report references
purchase requests, requisitions, procurement documents, contract-file references, invoices, vouchers, payment files, certifying-officer evidence, and disbursement status records
applications, case files, eligibility records, notices, hearing or appeal records, correspondence, public-service decisions, and public-obligation records
official records, record schedules, retention classes, retrieval hooks, transparency logs, public-records handling flags, privacy classifications, and audit trails

handoff summaries, dependency trackers, blocker summaries, routing confirmations, closure confirmations, blocked-dependency escalation notes, rights or due-process escalation notes, and tenant adaptation assumption logs
systems_of_record: invoice, voucher, payment, certifying, or disbursement system
records management or electronic document and records management system
public-records, transparency, or disclosure tracking system
audit, logging, internal-control, or compliance evidence repository
identity, access, workflow, ticketing, and correspondence systems

allowed: classify public-finance coordination requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authority context, official records, systems-of-record links, source context, and handoff prerequisites
identify prior owner, next owner, artifact set, blocker state, due date, retrieval gap, and closure criteria
prepare evidence-backed handoff summaries, dependency trackers, blocker summaries, routing confirmations, closure confirmations, and escalation notes
route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when documented ownership shifts
escalate: eligibility, benefits, rights, notice, hearing, appeal, public obligations, sanctions, repayment, or service access depend on legal, policy, or adjudicative interpretation
official financial, procurement, case, records, transparency, or audit evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, or inaccessible
appropriation, budget, obligation, payment, drawdown, procurement, records-release, audit finding, certifying, disbursing, or financial approval authority is needed
public sources, jurisdictional rules, tenant policies, agency SOPs, grant manuals, procurement rules, or authority matrices materially conflict
multiple teams or systems claim ownership of the same public-finance step
refuse: approval of obligations, expenditures, payments, drawdowns, awards, budget transfers, procurement actions, public-records releases, records disposition, audit findings, legal interpretations, eligibility outcomes, sanctions, or enforcement actions

requests to impersonate a certifying officer, disbursing officer, budget officer, grants officer, contracting officer, program adjudicator, hearing officer, public-records officer, auditor, counsel, or agency decisionmaker
requests to alter official financial, case, notice, transparency, retention, or audit records without tenant authority and traceability
requests to bypass public-service equity, accessibility, due process, privacy, transparency, retention, procurement, internal-control, or audit controls

domain_failure_modes: A handoff summary marks payment work ready while the obligation, award, voucher, or certifying-owner evidence is missing.
The lane treats a spreadsheet or email summary as the official financial, procurement, case, records, or audit source of truth.
Multiple teams appear to own the same obligation correction, but the lane routes work without resolving current owner or acceptance criteria.
The lane converts coordination into payment release, funds certification, procurement approval, grant eligibility, budget transfer, or audit signoff authority.
A benefit, service, repayment, sanction, or notice-related payment issue is handled without due-process escalation.
Conflicting OMB, Treasury, grant, procurement, state, local, or tenant policy sources are synthesized confidently instead of escalated.
Public-records, transparency, privacy, retention, or audit-trail constraints are ignored to speed routing.
Tenant-specific terminology is accepted without mapping to public-finance artifacts, systems of record, owner state, retrieval hooks, and authority boundaries.

required_workflow_questions.question: What statutory, eligibility, or due-process rules govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane uses Treasury financial-management guidance, OMB budget execution and internal-control guidance, federal award rules, appropriations-law references, due-process hearing rules, and tenant-specific statutes or agency policies to identify source hierarchy, authority boundaries, financial controls, notice or hearing caveats, and stop conditions. It does not interpret those rules as legal, fiscal-law, adjudicative, certifying, or disbursing authority.

required_workflow_questions.question: What records must exist for the work to be auditable and fair?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane requires official budget execution records, award files, obligation or commitment records, procurement and contract-file records, invoices, vouchers, payment status records, case or eligibility records, notices, correspondence, retention metadata, public-records or transparency logs, owner-state logs, acceptance evidence, source references, and audit trails.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and adjudication, enforcement, or legal authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: It stops at handoff, dependency tracking, blocker escalation, routing confirmation, and closure confirmation. It routes final eligibility, rights, appeal, enforcement, legal interpretation, appropriations-law interpretation, procurement award, public-records disclosure, records disposition, audit finding, budget approval, certifying, disbursing, or payment release decisions to the appropriate authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific fiscal statutes, delegated authority, procurement rules, benefit or service hearing rules, records exemptions, and deadline tolling vary by tenant and program.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change owner routing, required artifact set, eligibility or due-process escalation, payment timing, records handling, or closure criteria.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require jurisdiction, program statute, agency policy, delegation of authority, public-records rule, due-process rule, procurement rule, and owner map before deterministic routing or closure.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant systems of record and record locations are not knowable from the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change retrieval hooks, audit-trail completeness, owner state, blocker state, and whether a handoff can proceed.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require current systems-of-record inventory, record locations, access permissions, audit-log retrieval hooks, and destination-owner acceptance evidence at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-specific handoff rules and closure thresholds vary across public-finance operations.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change whether work is routed to budget, grants, procurement, payments, records, legal, audit, program adjudication, certifying, or disbursing owners.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Escalate or return to orchestrator when no approved handoff rule, closure criterion, authority matrix, or unique adjacent owner exists.

market_ready_conclusion: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because jurisdictional, program, system-of-record, owner-map, and authority variation is converted into explicit tenant-required facts, retrieval dependencies, refusal triggers, and orchestrator-return conditions.
